items.briefing:
Australia's communications regulator, the ACMA, has announced its compliance priorities for 2026–27, with a focus on protecting consumers from scams and harmful online content. This is relevant to registered training organisations, as it signals increased regulatory scrutiny around how organisations handle customer communications and data. Staff should be aware that scam activity targeting Australians remains high, with Scamwatch continuing to track a wide range of active threats.

Internationally, the Russian hacking group Gamaredon has upgraded its cyber tools, meaning organisations should ensure their security software is fully up to date. Additionally, a new global initiative is addressing risks from outdated open source software — a reminder that any older, unsupported software or systems in your workplace may carry hidden security risks.

**Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected email or message asking you to click a link or provide information, verify the request by contacting the sender directly through a known phone number before acting

featured.title: Local Police Collusion Hampers Crackdown on Asian Scam Centers
featured.quote: Local police corruption enabling Asian scam centers directly threatens Australian victims and enforcement efforts.
